messages.body:
Forward P/E of the biggest tech names right now: 
 
$AMZN 34x 
$AAPL 33x 
$GOOGL 30x 
$TSMC 26x 
$MSFT 24x 
 
Interesting split here. The leaders of the AI era are no longer trading on growth alone, but on how long they can sustain premium multiples. 
 
MSFT stands out as the lowest multiple among hyperscalers, while $AMZN and AAPL are still pricing in heavy expectations. 
 
At these levels, the real question is simple: who is actually cheap, and who is just expensive but “allowed” to stay expensive?

messages.body:
I really like $CPT 

Not that it’s high growth or even undervalued but b/c of its long runway.

I rented from them years ago in Atlanta & then helped someone move into one of their FL. properties.

They’ve got a nice system designed in which once you’re in (approved for an apt.), you can rent anywhere within their network and avoid new admin fees & the processing time.

It’s almost like their very own $AAPL esque ecosystem.  

Unlike $WU which I also like because of its LT history & runway, regardless of which view you take (5 day, monthly, 6M, 5yr or All Time) the return is positive except for CY.YTD & 1yr.

Fixable by EOY.

WU is the opposite.  

Pure discipline says avoid WU, especially since the divi payout ratio against Net Income is 70% (was 45% within the past year I believe) but based on one’s final cost basis, WU could print handsomely for years to come.

This Q is make or break; any further degradation to margin and we’ll most likely see a divi cut.

No point holding then.
